steps.title: Identify the temperature problem
steps.content: The passage explains that superfluorescence had only been observed at extremely cold temperatures because dipoles cannot synchronize at higher temperatures. The study needs a mechanism that allows synchronization at room temperature.

steps.title: Apply the polaron mechanism
steps.content: Polarons are described as shock-absorbing quasiparticles that protect dipoles from thermal interference. That protection would let dipoles synchronize despite higher temperatures. The answer is B.
